name: Ipci et al. Knowledge Profile Response Generation Extract
population_or_context: Open-ended logical-proof responses in a discrete mathematics course.
sample_size: 585 authentic open-ended logical-proof responses used as the real-response comparator.
methods: Extracted the LLM synthetic response generation methodology, knowledge-profile conditioning, representative-example use, and reported profile-based generation strategies.
education_context: Higher-education discrete mathematics logical-proof responses with analytic rubric alignment.
response_origin: Synthetic responses generated by LLMs and compared with authentic open-ended student responses.
rubric_label_space: Rubric-aligned response quality categories for logical proof work.
label_source: Knowledge profiles and representative examples guide generation; authentic response labels and rubrics provide the comparison boundary.
generation_model: Large language models; model details are not fully specified in the repository metadata page.
generation_method: Profile-based LLM generation guided by knowledge profiles and representative examples.
prompt_strategy: Two profile-based strategies are reported: straightforward profile use and error-informed profile use.
evaluation_method: Diversity and realism analysis with embedding-based distance metrics and PCA, plus utility for training automated grading models.
scorer_use: Training augmentation and formative-feedback model development; not direct validation of a scorer on synthetic-only data.
real_response_comparator: Authentic logical-proof responses from the same educational context are used as the comparator.
reported_metrics: Repository page reports N = 585 authentic responses and discusses diversity, realism, and model-training utility.
limitations: The public metadata page summarizes the method but does not expose every prompt detail.
The source is bounded to discrete mathematics logical-proof responses.
Synthetic responses alone reportedly perform worse than real data for model training.
